facts.context: Which tool will best help scientists understand relationships between oceans and climate changes?
What is one way the body reacts to cold weather?
In the water cycle, where does water go after reaching the ocean?
facts.truth: The water changes into water vapor.
distractors: The water flows into a river.
The water falls from the clouds.
The water sinks to the ocean floor.

distractors: types of fossils the rocks contain
number of minerals found in the rocks

In which layer of the atmosphere does most of Earth's weather occur?
facts.truth: the troposphere, which is the lowest layer
distractors: the exosphere, which is the outermost layer
the thermosphere, which has the highest temperature
the stratosphere, which contains a sub-layer of ozone

facts.context: A student placed a rock in a graduated cylinder containing water, causing the water level in the cylinder to increase by 20 mL. This increase represents the rock's
George is studying erosion. He learns how rocks break apart over time. Which of these most likely forms when a rock weathers and breaks into small pieces?

Many scientists accept the theory that excess emission of carbon dioxide from cars and industry causes a layer of gas in the upper atmosphere that traps heat. This, in turn, causes the average temperature on Earth to rise. Which of the following observations supports that theory?
facts.truth: Carbon dioxide in the upper atmosphere reflects heat radiated from Earth's surface.
distractors: The summer of 1998 was one of the hottest summer seasons on record.
Carbon dioxide gas from cars and industry is a product of burning that produces heat.
Radiation on Earth's surface is increasing because of a hole in the ozone layer

facts.context: Sandstone and all other types of sedimentary rock are made up of particles. The source materials of these particles consist of
facts.truth: fragments that broke off of older rocks.
distractors: magma that cooled deep below a volcano.
gases that condensed in the atmosphere.
vegetation that decayed in thick layers.

facts.context: Which statement describes a property of water that most likely causes large rocks to break apart?
distractors: Water forms droplets as it falls.
Water turns into a gas when it boils.
Water takes the shape of its container.

facts.context: What force causes objects to be pulled toward Earth?
Tides occur in the oceans at regular times every day because of
facts.truth: the gravitational pull of the Sun and the Moon.
distractors: the tilt of Earth's rotational axis.
density differences caused by temperature changes.

facts.context: Penny's class was learning how rapidly some changes can happen on Earth. Which event causes the fastest change to the surface of Earth?
distractors: a wave crashing on a beach

facts.context: If the Earth rotated in the opposite direction, what would most likely occur?
facts.truth: The Sun would rise in the west.
distractors: There would be more days in each year.
The phases of the Moon would last longer.

facts.context: Tectonic motion caused tension between plates in Earth's crust. A sudden release of this energy occurred. Based on this information, which event happened?
Hurricanes are driven by the energy they get from ocean water. Which property of water allows it to retain the energy needed to power a hurricane?
An earthquake is caused by sudden shifts in which of the following layers of Earth?
The news often talks about how global warming could change environmental conditions on Earth. Which best explains why global warming is such a concern?
How long does it take light from the nearest star other than the Sun to reach Earth?
Which layer of Earth is divided into plates?
At certain times of the year, meteor showers can be observed from Earth. Why are these objects from space usually harmless?
facts.truth: They burn up high in Earth's atmosphere
distractors: They are closer to the sun than to Earth.
They are made of soft, spongy materials.

facts.context: What instrument should be used to monitor atmospheric pressure changes as a weather system approaches?

How would the Sun appear to an astronaut on Pluto compared with how it looks from Earth?
facts.truth: The Sun would appear smaller and dimmer.
distractors: The Sun would appear larger and brighter.
The Sun would appear smaller and brighter.
The Sun would appear larger and dimmer.

facts.context: Patricia and her classmates are visiting different rocky areas in the city. They want to know what kind of rocks can be found in each area. If the investigation is done correctly, what will Patricia and her classmates do each time they visit an area?
facts.truth: record the rock types and locations
distractors: measure the rock masses and lengths
